page: Amazon Web Services c9g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. This instance features an arm64 CPU architecture with 16 dedicated physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a single thread per core, the system provides 16 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

The c9g.4xlarge instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware. This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high compute density per resource. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance appropriate for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Suitable workloads include batch processing, distributed analytics, web serving, and containerized microservices that benefit from dedicated physical cores and arm64 optimization.
description: Amazon Web Services c9g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the arm64 architecture and powered by AWS Graviton processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and a single thread per core, providing 16 vCPUs. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and containerized microservices that require dedicated CPU resources without a large memory footprint.
